frameworks.name: biophysical working memory models
frameworks.one_line: Circuit-level WM models used to interpret tDCS effects on PFC.

frameworks.name: excitation-inhibition (E/I) balance
frameworks.one_line: Intermediate level linking currents to WM via cortical balance.

frameworks.name: persistent activity coding
frameworks.one_line: Attractor-like sustained firing as WM substrate, with dynamical limits.

frameworks.name: gamma oscillatory activity
frameworks.one_line: Oscillatory index tied to E/I balance and WM performance.

core_question: Can biophysical WM models inform and reconcile mixed results of prefrontal tDCS effects on working memory?
key_assumptions: Scalp tDCS modulates prefrontal cortical excitability
Multilevel mapping from currents to behavior is necessary
E/I balance is a tractable intermediate level
manipulations: Reviewed prefrontal tDCS protocols and WM outcomes
Articulated levels of analysis from currents to circuit to behavior
key_findings: Empirical tDCS-WM literature shows mixed and inconsistent results
Possible preferential effect on WM control functions
interpretations: Oversimplified excitability assumptions need revision via circuit models
Persistent-activity coding dynamics limit how E/I shifts translate to WM
limitations_noted: Dynamical aspects of persistent-activity coding constrain inferences
